primary_reason: The questionnaire describes healthcare workers' needlestick practices and attitudes using standard descriptive statistics and regression without developing a transferable measurement or surveillance method.
This review synthesizes animal neuropathology without contributing a transferable epidemiological method or infectious-population application.
This review compares laboratory detections with mandatory notifications to quantify psittacosis surveillance completeness.
This study validates maternal recall as an alternative method for vaccination-coverage measurement.
This study links hepatitis surveillance and death records to quantify mortality patterns and death-certificate underreporting.
This case-based review concerns immune-checkpoint-inhibitor renal toxicity in cancer and does not provide a concrete infectious-disease methodological transfer route.
This study nests serology within a coverage survey to compare reported vaccination with measured population immunity.
This general childhood mortality trend study lacks an explicit transferable infectious-disease methodological contribution.
This audit estimates true immunization coverage and diagnoses underreporting, upload errors, and duplicate registry records.
This decision model estimates the health benefits, costs, and uncertainty of routine yellow-fever vaccination.
The review addresses smoking, alcohol, and diabetes, with infections mentioned only as one component of alcohol-related morbidity and no concrete infectious-disease method.
This laboratory imaging-flow-cytometry assay study lacks a transferable population epidemiological method or application.
This study validates parental vaccination recall against electronic records and identifies accuracy correlates.
The study describes clinical and laboratory manifestations among patients meeting an expanded-dengue definition without contributing a reusable design or measurement method.

The spontaneous-report case series describes Fournier gangrene associated with diabetes medicines but does not evaluate or correct the reporting system or establish a transferable epidemiologic method.
This mechanistic transmission model estimates contributions from hospitals, community carriers, infants, and animal reservoirs.
This study estimates antibody-response kinetics specifically to enable cross-sectional seroincidence measurement across surveillance settings.
The narrative review summarizes Borrelia miyamotoi ecology, clinical presentation, diagnosis, and knowledge gaps without substantively developing or comparing epidemiologic methods.
This study integrates serology, outbreak testing, and sentinel surveillance to expose fever-rash underreporting and misdiagnosis.
The report describes one daptomycin-resistant Corynebacterium striatum infection and its mutations without a transferable surveillance or analytic design.
This review compares blood-safety surveillance interventions, screening results, and underrecognition of transfusion-transmitted flavivirus infections.
The case-control analysis concerns diet and central nervous system demyelination, and adjustment for infectious mononucleosis does not provide an infectious-disease methodological transfer route.
The systematic review evaluates reconstructive surgical missions and follow-up quality without an infectious-disease outcome or concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The retrospective student survey describes bloodborne-pathogen exposure reporting and reasons for nonreporting without evaluating a reusable measurement or correction method.
The report describes vaccination coverage, supplementary activities, and reported measles incidence in Pakistan without introducing a transferable analytic or surveillance-adjustment method.
This validation links clinical and surveillance records to quantify hepatitis underreporting and associated patient characteristics.
This study benchmarks influenza hospitalization forecasts using transmission modelling, data assimilation, backcasting, and search-based nowcasting.
This data-quality assessment evaluates immunization reporting accuracy, completeness, timeliness, and monitoring-system performance across facilities.
The review summarizes African Campylobacter prevalence and antimicrobial resistance but does not substantively compare or prescribe epidemiologic methods.
The mixed-methods survey describes needlestick knowledge and reporting barriers among junior doctors without developing a transferable measurement or surveillance method.
This reporting-bias study concerns repeat pregnancy and provides no explicit infectious-disease transfer route.
The study describes reported congenital cytomegalovirus cases and their association with maternal infection type but does not directly estimate or correct surveillance completeness.
This burden study corrects underreported and misclassified deaths to estimate infectious and other childhood mortality trends.
This commentary provides literature-based guidance for replacing deficient passive vaccine-safety reporting with active surveillance.
This contraceptive measurement study is outside infectious epidemiology and offers no explicit infectious transfer route.
This study compares mortality and surveillance data to estimate underreporting of fatal congenital Zika syndrome.
The claims analysis concerns injuries from central venous access devices, and infection is only one complication within a non-infectious patient-safety outcome.
This study compares administrative and mandatory surveillance data to quantify syphilis underreporting and propose improvement.
This study combines surveillance and follow-up data to estimate outbreak costs, disability, and underreporting uncertainty.
This review pools validation measures to diagnose infection-surveillance misclassification and assess effects of definition changes.
This study combines human serology, rodent sampling, and spatial risk analysis to investigate leptospirosis transmission.
The analysis describes demographic and geographic influenza-mortality disparities using routine data but does not correct the acknowledged racial misclassification or underreporting.
The study estimates underreporting of snakebite envenomation, which is outside infectious diseases and supplies no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The literature review summarizes Caribbean leptospirosis epidemiology, serovars, and surveillance gaps without substantively evaluating or prescribing a method.
The case-control analysis concerns fish intake and demyelination, and adjustment for infectious mononucleosis does not create a concrete infectious-disease method.
This assessment applies standardized verification metrics to diagnose quality problems in immunization and related administrative data.
This field assessment diagnoses linkage-to-care tracking failures and identifies record identifiers and protocols that improve monitoring.
This pilot applies a transferable automated electronic case-reporting surveillance design.
This mechanistic transmission model estimates leishmaniasis reproduction, host-specific transmission, underreporting, and combined control effects.
This study develops a clinical diagnostic score for leptospirosis and reports its discrimination and operating characteristics.
This modelling study integrates burden estimates, spatial mapping, and transmission ensembles to evaluate Wolbachia deployment.
This study links hepatitis surveillance and hospital records to monitor complications, incidence, coding underreporting, and linkage uncertainty.
This study uses implant retrieval records to quantify infection-related revision timing and follow-up underascertainment.
This study integrates multiple health databases with targeted review to improve surgical-site infection ascertainment.
The machine-learning analysis corrects suicide classification among drug-overdose deaths and provides no concrete transfer route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
This study combines surveillance sources and capture-recapture estimation to assess tick-borne encephalitis underreporting.
This study analyzes global surveillance completeness, vaccination coverage, and age patterns to characterize diphtheria epidemiology.
This study links three HIV registers and applies capture-recapture models with mortality-underreporting adjustment.
The study validates self-reported alcohol use with a biomarker among women living with HIV, but alcohol use rather than an infectious outcome is the measurement target.
This protocol integrates syndromic signals, point-process modelling, targeted diagnostics, genomics, and economic evaluation.
This systematic review diagnoses consequential offspring-reporting deficiencies in trials including HIV.
This appraisal diagnoses underreporting, discordance, and documentation weaknesses in rabies and animal-bite surveillance.
This outbreak investigation combines active case finding, interviews, environmental inspection, microbiology, and genomic sequencing.
This study links hepatitis surveillance and transplant registries to estimate incidence, predictors, survival, and underreporting.
This study quantifies surgical-site infection underascertainment caused by single administrative sources and short follow-up.
The review concerns opioid-induced endocrine disorders and supplies no infectious-disease outcome or concrete transfer route.
The mixed-methods study describes child mortality, recent illnesses, and cultural perceptions in rural Nepal without developing a reusable infectious-disease measurement method.
This study evaluates transferable verbal surveillance measurements against serological infection evidence.
This study develops and simulation-evaluates cumulative-incidence estimation under differential outcome misclassification using validation data.
This applied study calibrates a mechanistic age-structured influenza transmission model.
The interview study primarily measures childhood sexual experiences, psychological distress, and substance use, with HIV status only a participant characteristic.
This study estimates post-measles complication risk while explicitly adjusting denominators for surveillance underreporting.
The provider survey describes coccidioidomycosis knowledge and reporting awareness without validating a surveillance measure or correction procedure.
The surgical database analysis concerns parotidectomy outcomes and reconstruction, with surgical-site infection only one postoperative complication.
This mixed-methods assessment diagnoses adverse-event underreporting using records, medication proxies, and direct clinical observation.
The report presents three neonatal Elizabethkingia infection or colonization cases without evaluating a transferable diagnostic or surveillance method.
This evaluation applies surveillance-system criteria to diagnose weaknesses in sensitivity, timeliness, reporting, and stability.
This linkage study diagnoses missing and discordant treatment, outcome, and mortality data across TB and ART registers.
The analysis measures depression and psychosocial correlates among participants in an HIV pre-exposure-prophylaxis trial rather than an infectious outcome or infectious surveillance process.
This study applies record comparison and qualitative investigation to tuberculosis underreporting.
This laboratory epitope-identification study develops assay components without a transferable epidemiological method or application.
The review concerns immune-mediated post-transfusion purpura and does not identify a concrete transfer route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
This study develops a multilayer dynamic HCV transmission model for hidden-population estimation and intervention evaluation.
This article provides substantive surveillance, reporting, preparedness, and response guidance for emerging aquatic-animal diseases.
This study compares four register sources to diagnose norovirus underreporting and possible case misclassification.
This comparative clinical study evaluates culture-guided prophylaxis and predictors of post-biopsy sepsis.
This pharmacovigilance description concerns adverse reactions without an infectious-disease transfer route.
This register analysis diagnoses norovirus underascertainment caused by limited laboratory testing during gastroenteritis outbreaks.
This study develops and evaluates machine-learning surveillance for detecting potentially pathogen-contaminated foods from consumer reports.
This early-outbreak analysis uses likelihood-based exponential modelling to estimate unreported coronavirus cases and transmissibility.
This review assesses foodborne outbreak investigations and diagnoses underreporting, laboratory, and production-practice deficiencies.
This published erratum supplies bibliographic correction information for the associated norovirus-underreporting article.
This feasibility study evaluates user acceptance and design requirements for mobile vaccine adverse-event surveillance.
This study compares diagnostic approaches for estimating an animal parasite's prevalence.
This study applies a double list experiment to reduce social-desirability bias in HIV-related condom-use estimates.
The systematic review catalogs equine zoonotic pathogens, transmission routes, and risk factors without substantively evaluating an epidemiologic method.
The study measures occupational ultraviolet exposure for skin-cancer prevention and provides no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This study demonstrates routine-data linkage for correcting severe underascertainment in national melioidosis surveillance.
The review chapter summarizes Buruli-ulcer history, burden, evolution, transmission uncertainty, and pathogenesis without developing or evaluating a transferable method.
This data article documents a longitudinal dengue cohort dataset and its active case-ascertainment procedures for reuse.
This study applies Bayesian adjustment to quantify how HBV underreporting changes vaccination effect estimates.
The review summarizes immune evasion and pathogenesis in Mycobacterium ulcerans and M. leprae without a transferable epidemiologic-method contribution.
This study predicts opioid involvement in overdose deaths without an explicit infectious-disease transfer route.
The survey describes needlestick incidence, nonreporting, and opinions about high-risk announcements without evaluating the proposed intervention or a reusable surveillance method.
This nonresearch study guide summarizes lipoid pneumonia diagnosis and management but contributes no infectious-disease method, diagnostic evaluation, or reproducibility resource.
This review identifies laboratory-acquired infection reporting deficiencies and provides surveillance and prevention guidance.
This study diagnoses and corrects screening, test, and reporting biases in national chlamydia trends.
This study applies transferable cost-effectiveness and sensitivity analyses to pertussis vaccination.
The review concerns asbestos-related cancers and provides no concrete route to infectious-disease epidemiologic methods.
This descriptive opioid hospitalization analysis lacks an explicit transferable infectious-disease epidemiological contribution.
This qualitative study diagnoses operational weaknesses and underreporting mechanisms within a dengue surveillance system.
The review addresses chronic obstructive pulmonary disease assessment and treatment, with respiratory infections mentioned only as exacerbation triggers.
This study applies demographic underreporting adjustment and regression-based cause reassignment to estimate HIV mortality trends.

The clinic-record study reports associations between nationality, economic period, and sexually transmitted infection diagnoses using standard cross-sectional analyses without a reusable design contribution.
The surveillance analysis describes healthcare workers experiencing biological-material accidents but neither measures underreporting nor evaluates a correction or reporting intervention.
The systematic review summarizes reported chikungunya circulation in Africa but the abstract identifies no substantive comparison or prescription of methods.
The reproductive-age mortality survey estimates maternal deaths and their causes, with infection only one obstetric cause rather than an infectious-disease methodological target.
This study evaluates alternative NLP-enhanced machine-learning specifications for electronic surgical-site infection surveillance.
This article develops modified distance sampling to estimate spatial underreporting of clinic-detected infectious cases.
The household survey estimates induced abortion and complications, with possible infection being a procedural complication rather than an infectious-disease outcome.
This study models yellow-fever transmission, underreporting, vaccine demand, and intervention impact across Africa.
This historical epidemiological study triangulates archival sources to examine measles severity, underreporting, and social determinants.
The article comments on conflict-related vulnerabilities and proposes broad COVID-19 capacity improvements in Syria without presenting a substantive analytic method.
This modelling synthesis diagnoses limitations of zero reported cases as an elimination indicator and identifies needed analyses.
This study combines transmission modelling, inverse uncertainty quantification, and sensitivity analysis across three COVID-19 settings.
The cross-sectional pharmacy survey describes COVID-19 preparedness and reporting behavior without evaluating a transferable surveillance intervention or correction method.
This study applies coordinated pharmacovigilance, causality assessment, and incidence estimation to COVID-19 treatment harms.
This study integrates laboratory and surveillance data to assess rabies incidence, regional underreporting, and response needs.
This review identifies Rift Valley fever surveillance gaps and provides outbreak risk-mitigation guidance.
The questionnaire estimates occupational blood-and-body-fluid exposures and reasons for nonreporting but does not evaluate a reporting intervention or correction method.
This study develops an inference approach controlling surveillance changes and underreporting when estimating epidemic growth.
This study applies mortality-based correction to estimate COVID-19 surveillance underdetection.
This study applies decision modelling to compare integrated dengue prevention strategies.
This study extends and evaluates epidemic re-emergence estimators using analytical and stochastic infectious-disease simulations.
The registry analysis estimates overall cancer mortality, and its mention of vaccine-preventable cancers does not establish an infectious-disease measurement contribution.
The review develops bioinformatic strategies for hereditary-cancer splice variants and offers no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The incident-report analysis concerns general pediatric medication errors, with antimicrobials and vaccines only two drug categories within a non-infectious patient-safety outcome.
This record provides and evaluates a decentralized rabies diagnostic protocol with sensitivity assessment and subsequent molecular surveillance capability.
This narrative disease-control review lacks a distinct transferable epidemiological methods contribution.
This study applies a transferable feasibility assessment to infection surveillance measures.
This retrospective policy evaluation assesses whether mandatory environmental monitoring detects relevant Legionella risk and prevents legionellosis.
The cohort primarily evaluates colorectal-surgery readmissions, with deep infection only one cause of later readmission rather than the methodological target.
The narrative review systematically searches and summarizes meningococcal burden, serogroups, and vaccination recommendations without substantively evaluating an epidemiologic method.
The pilot develops an integrated surveillance system specifically for noncommunicable diseases and provides no concrete infectious-disease implementation or validation.
This study uses counterfactual mortality forecasts to estimate COVID-19 underreporting.
The article discusses examples and possible reasons for COVID-19 underreporting in Turkey without presenting a substantive estimation or diagnostic method.
The historical review summarizes canine parvovirus epidemiology, surveillance, and knowledge gaps in Australia without substantively evaluating a reusable method.
This study evaluates reliability and feasibility of a standardized infection-surveillance tool.
The report describes declining measles notifications during the COVID-19 pandemic and raises possible underdiagnosis without estimating or correcting it.
This study applies repeated data-quality assessments to infectious-disease surveillance reporting.
